macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Breaks Major Support Level, 10% Further Decline Likely
items.summary: The S&P 500 has broken a key support level, with equity-only put-call ratios rising bearishly and market breadth weakening. Analysts warn of another 10% drop ahead amid ongoing sell signals.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Prepares for Wave 3 Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Technical analysis indicates Bitcoin is positioning for a potential Wave 3 movement, with traders monitoring divergence signals and RSI indicators. The cryptocurrency is showing resilience despite broader market corrections in equities.

items.title: Oil Price Surge Triggers Broader Market Repricing Concerns
items.summary: WTI crude surged approximately 9% in a single day to around $96, while Brent exceeded $100 per barrel following geopolitical tensions. Energy disruptions are creating supply and financing shocks affecting crypto and stock markets.

items.title: Market Liquidation Mode Escalates as Indices Decline
items.summary: Database models entered full liquidation mode with Dow Jones down 1.5-1.7% and Nasdaq down 1.7%, potentially triggering further selling pressure. Intermediate models could drop below 48%, signaling increased market vulnerability.

items.title: Tech Sector Correction Continues with Leveraged ETF Losses
items.summary: Leveraged tech ETF TQQ declined approximately 5% in a single day to $46, reflecting broader equity market weakness amid geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Announces $100M Claude Partner Network and New Chart Generation Features
items.summary: Anthropic launched a $100 million investment in its Claude Partner Network to boost enterprise adoption and added capabilities for generating custom charts, diagrams, and visualizations. The company is also embroiled in a legal battle with the Pentagon over surveillance concerns.[1]

items.title: Google Integrates Gemini AI into Maps and Launches Task Automation
items.summary: Google Maps now features a conversational 'Ask Maps' interface powered by Gemini, while Gemini task automation for ordering coffee and booking appointments goes live on Samsung S26 and Pixel 10 devices. Google has not ruled out introducing ads to Gemini.[1]

items.title: Microsoft Launches Copilot Health and Facebook Adds AI Auto-Replies
items.summary: Microsoft introduced Copilot Health, which connects to medical records and wearables, amid growing AI job interview bots affecting millions. Facebook Marketplace now uses AI for auto-replies.[1]

items.title: Morgan Stanley Predicts AI-Driven Surge in Three Job Types
items.summary: Despite AI reducing headcount in some areas, Morgan Stanley identifies three job categories seeing increased demand due to AI advancements.[2]

items.title: Google Uses Gemini to Predict Flash Floods from News Reports
items.summary: Google researchers employed Gemini to analyze 5 million news articles, creating a geo-tagged dataset of 2.6 million floods called 'Groundsource' to improve flash flood predictions.[3]

items.title: NIH-Funded AI Tool Predicts Intimate Partner Violence Risk
items.summary: Researchers from Harvard developed multimodal machine learning models using routine medical data to accurately identify patients at risk of intimate partner violence, aiding clinicians in support.[4]

items.title: Nvidia GTC 2026 Preview: Groq Acquisition and Robotics Focus
items.summary: Nvidia's upcoming GTC event may highlight integration of Groq's dataflow architecture acquired for $20B, alongside advancements in robotics via Isaac GR00T and Omniverse.[5]

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Wins Game of the Year at 26th Game Developers Choice Awards
items.summary: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 dominated the 26th Annual Game Developers Choice Awards, securing five wins including Game of the Year, Best Debut, Best Visual Art, and Best Narrative. Blue Prince won Best Design and Innovation, while Death Stranding 2 took Best Technology; Don Daglow received the Lifetime Achievement Award.[1]

items.title: Xsolla Releases Report on Future Opportunities for Video Game Developers
items.summary: Xsolla published a new industry report highlighting AI reducing development timelines by 20% and support costs by 40%, the rise of 'platform omnivore' gamers to 43% in the US, and growth in live service models like PUBG and Roblox. It outlines priorities like modularity, owned audience infrastructure, and compliance for global markets.[2]

items.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Officially Announced with Full Schedule
items.summary: Summer Game Fest 2026 has been announced, including its event schedule and expected titles, plus the return of The Game Business Live on June 8 focusing on market trends, subscriptions, and funding in gaming.[5]

items.title: Blizzard Veteran Rob Pardo: 'Love' is Key Game Development Milestone
items.summary: At GDC Festival of Gaming, Bonfire Studios co-founder Rob Pardo emphasized 'love' as the most important milestone in game development, drawing from his experience on StarCraft, World of Warcraft, and Diablo.[6]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Major Tech Companies Pledge to Power Own AI Data Centers
items.summary: Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, and xAI have signed a federal pledge to supply their own power for AI data centers, aimed at limiting the impact of AI infrastructure on consumer electricity costs.

items.title: Helium Supply Crunch Impacts Asian Chipmakers
items.summary: Asian chipmakers are facing significant challenges due to helium supply constraints, with Chinese chip designers experiencing market pressure as they compete with Nvidia and Huawei.

items.title: Google Develops AI System to Predict Flash Floods Using Historical News Reports
items.summary: Google is leveraging large language models and historical news data to create a flash flood prediction system designed for regions lacking expensive weather infrastructure and meteorological records.

items.title: Alibaba Launches New Open Cloud Mobile App
items.summary: Alibaba has announced the launch of a new mobile application for its open cloud platform, representing the next phase of AI adoption beyond chat interfaces.
